question: For root fracture it is imperative to take X-ray at?

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: Root fractures can be challenging to diagnose clinically due to their location within bone. Different imaging angles may provide better visualization depending on the specific part of the tooth involved. Taking multiple x-rays from different angles (such as mesial-distal, buccolingual, occluso-gingival planes) increases diagnostic accuracy for detecting these subtle injuries.

The correct option that includes all necessary angles is D: 45°, 90°, 110° which allows comprehensive evaluation of potential root fractures. Answer Choice D: Yes. Based on my analysis, I choose
Answer: D

answer.raw_response:
Root fractures can be challenging to diagnose clinically due to their location within bone. Different imaging angles may provide better visualization depending on the specific part of the tooth involved. Taking multiple x-rays from different angles (such as mesial-distal, buccolingual, occluso-gingival planes) increases diagnostic accuracy for detecting these subtle injuries.

The correct option that includes all necessary angles is D: 45°, 90°, 110° which allows comprehensive evaluation of potential root fractures. Answer Choice D: Yes. Based on my analysis, I choose Option D: 45°, 90°, 110°. This set of angles provides the best coverage needed for diagnosing possible root fractures by allowing multi-plane imaging perspectives. Comprehensive assessment with these views maximizes detection rates while minimizing missed diagnoses. Therefore, I select D as the optimal answer.
